title: Friday Ride to Skov
objective: Ride home-to-cottage as the week's aerobic anchor: a long, fully aerobic point-to-point from St-Lambert to Austin that turns the commute to Skov into durability volume - the right kind of load for a re-entry week.
why_now: Friday PM in summer is the high-value window, and a long Z2 ride is the safest way to add load after a disrupted, manual-labor fortnight - far better than intensity. The route to the cottage is hilly through the Townships, so the discipline is capping every climb and fueling early; this is a deliberate long-endurance day, not a tempo ride. Weather-gated: if conditions are bad, drive and ride locally or rest instead.

source_context.week_objective: Re-entry and aerobic rebuild after a disrupted, manual-labor weekend

route.terrain: rolling South Shore to hilly Eastern Townships near Orford/Memphremagog; real climbing in the back half
route.notes: Plan a cycling route that avoids autoroutes (e.g. Chambly/Marieville/Granby/Eastman corridor). Carry food, layers, lights, and a plan to refill bottles.

targets.notes: 120-140 km fully aerobic at 56-70% FTP (~145-182 W) on the flats. Conversational the whole way. Climbs will spike power - shift down, stay seated when you can, and cap the surges; do not let the hills turn this into a tempo grind. No structured intervals.

warmup.notes: Roll out of St-Lambert easy; let the legs and back settle before the terrain stands up.

steps.notes: Z2 hour: fuel and drink on schedule, cap every climb (shift down, stay seated), reset posture.
Final 30 min into Austin - ease the effort and finish relaxed.

cooldown.notes: Spin the last few km into Austin easy.

nutrition.pre: Full carb-forward breakfast 2-3h before, ~2g carbs/kg. Top up 30-40g carbs in the last hour before rolling.
nutrition.during: 60-90g carbs/hour from the start - this is a 4-5h ride, so real food plus drink mix, not just gels. Drink to thirst plus electrolytes; plan a store/refill stop. Do not ride the back-half climbs underfueled.
nutrition.post: Big recovery meal: 30-40g protein plus generous carbs, then keep grazing through the evening at the cottage.
nutrition.centr_recipe: High-protein Lentil Braise with Flatbread, Centr collection link: https://centr.com/blog/show/32824/10-high-protein-recipes-for-easy-meal-prep
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: electrolytes/sodium across the ride, especially if hot. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Refuel hard, rehydrate, legs-up and the left-calf routine in the evening.
recovery.evening: Easy cottage evening; protect sleep after a long day in the saddle.
recovery.next_morning_check: Saturday is recovery by feel. If the legs/back are cooked, make Saturday a short flush or full rest; only ride longer if you genuinely feel good.

skill_focus.action: On every climb, shift early and rebuild power smoothly over the crest instead of punching over the top; keep surges under ~78% FTP.
skill_focus.why: Capping the Townships climbs is what keeps a long ride aerobic and protects the back and the rest of the cottage week - the durability comes from steady hours, not from attacking hills.

skill_focus.action: Reset posture every 20-30 min: loosen the grip, drop the shoulders, shift hand position.
skill_focus.why: Position fatigue on a 4-5h ride is what tightens the back and left calf; small resets keep you durable late.

mental_focus.intention: Long and patient, all the way to Skov.
mental_focus.why: The point is hours of clean aerobic load and arriving at the cottage tired-but-fueled, not a fast time or KOMs on the climbs.
mental_focus.reset: If you start chasing a climb or a segment, ease off the crest, eat or drink if due, and settle back below the cap for two minutes.

pre_interval_cues: Weather-gated: if it is storming or unsafe, drive and ride local or rest - do not force a 5h ride in bad conditions.
Carry food, layers, lights, and a refill plan.
Aerobic all day, cap the climbs.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Good - leave it aerobic. The distance is the stimulus; do not add tempo on the climbs.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Shorten the route, stop to eat and reset, or drive the final leg if the back or legs are done - this is endurance, not a sufferfest. No guilt in cutting it.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Arrived at Skov having stayed conversational and well-fueled, capped the climbs, with the back no worse than at the start.
